claim: `hotam apply-proposal` shall resolve a Conflict by its string ID when locating the Conflict node a proposal addresses, whether the ID is given directly or via a variable-style alias.
why: The C-8600b1b8 lesson: in the pre-port Python graph, all six Conflict nodes in domains/hotam-spec-self/graph.py bound axis/context to local variables (c1_axis..c6_axis / c1_ctx..c6_ctx), so literal-only matching in _find_conflict_call made every existing Conflict unaddressable and the standing P3 CONFLICT_STALLED action mechanically unlandable; the fix folded module/function-level `name = "literal"` assignments. In the Go port this concern is structurally moot: the graph is JSON (graph.json), not executable Python source, so Conflict nodes carry plain string IDs with no variable indirection. findConflictIndex (internal/proposal/mutate.go) locates a conflict by exact ID match on the JSON graph -- no variable resolution is needed. This repairs the ACT half of the mediation loop for conflict transitions (R-active-loop-apply-tool).
